title: `podman machine list` can't evaluate multiple format fields
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Trying to get the  username, the path to ssh identify file and the SSH Port to use to connect to VM for testing with Ansible.
See https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-machine-list.1.html for valid placeholders for the Go template.

`podman machine ls --format json` should for example output a json document but actually return the string `json`.

`{{.Port}}` should return the SSH Port to use to connect to VM according to the document, but returns `podman-machine-default*Error: template: list:1:23: executing "list" at <.Port>: can't evaluate field Port in type *machine.machineReporter`.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```sh
$ podman machine list --format "{{.Name}}\t{{.Port}}\n"
podman-machine-default*Error: template: list:1:23: executing "list" at <.Port>: can't evaluate field Port in type *machine.machineReporter
$ podman machine list --format "{{.Name}}\t{{.RemoteUsername}}\t{{.Port}}\n"
podman-machine-default*Error: template: list:1:23: executing "list" at <.RemoteUsername>: can't evaluate field RemoteUsername in type *machine.machineReporter
$  podman machine list --format "{{.Name}}\t{{.VMType}}"
podman-machine-default*  qemu
$ podman machine list --format "{{.Name}}\t{{.RemoteUsername}}\t{{.Port}}"
podman-machine-default*Error: template: list:1:23: executing "list" at <.RemoteUsername>: can't evaluate field RemoteUsername in type *machine.machineReporter
$ podman machine ls --format json
json
$ podman machine ls --format "table {{.Name}}\t{{.VMType}}\t{{.Created}}\t{{.LastUp}}"
NAME                     VM TYPE     CREATED      LAST UP
podman-machine-default*  qemu        12 days ago  Currently running
$ 
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.17.3
Built:        Wed Dec  8 19:41:11 2021
OS/Arch:      darwin/amd64

Server:
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Wed Dec  8 22:45:07 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

Yes

comments.body:
Fixed in the main branch,

$ ./bin/podman machine ls --format json
[
    {
        "Name": "podman-machine-default",
        "Default": false,
        "Created": "2021-12-27T10:36:14.373347492-05:00",
        "Running": false,
        "LastUp": "2021-12-27T11:22:50.17333371-05:00",
        "Stream": "default",
        "VMType": "qemu",
        "CPUs": 1,
        "Memory": "2147483648",
        "DiskSize": "10737418240",
        "Port": 38243,
        "RemoteUsername": "core",
        "IdentityPath": "/home/dwalsh/.ssh/podman-machine-default"
    }
]
Should be in 4.0.
